title-group.article-title: Blood-brain barrier transport in Alzheimer disease models

abstract.p: Blood-brain barrier transport mechanisms influence amyloid-beta movement between blood and brain compartments.
Model studies suggest that transporter balance, endothelial injury, and inflammatory signaling can change amyloid-beta exposure in Alzheimer disease contexts.

sec.title: Results
sec.p: RAGE and LRP1 showed opposing directional roles in simplified blood-brain barrier transport models.
